Now let me solve this carefully, enumerating all cases.

## Setup

Three independent sensors with detection probabilities:
- LIDAR: P(L) = 0.995, P(L') = 0.005
- Radar: P(R) = 0.98, P(R') = 0.02
- Camera: P(C) = 0.95, P(C') = 0.05

System **fails** when **fewer than 2** sensors detect — i.e., exactly 0 or exactly 1 detect.

## P(exactly 0 detect) — all three fail

P(L' ∩ R' ∩ C') = 0.005 × 0.02 × 0.05 = **0.000005**

## P(exactly 1 detects) — three sub-cases

| Case | Calculation | Result |
|---|---|---|
| Only LIDAR detects | 0.995 × 0.02 × 0.05 | 0.000995 |
| Only Radar detects | 0.005 × 0.98 × 0.05 | 0.000245 |
| Only Camera detects | 0.005 × 0.02 × 0.95 | 0.000095 |

Sum of exactly-1 cases: **0.001335**

## Total failure probability

P(fail) = 0.000005 + 0.001335 = **0.001340**

As a percentage: 0.001340 × 100 = **0.1340%**
